dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 8442 IH: Patient Refunds for Bad Denials Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Ms. Craig (for herself and Mr. Ryan) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce

form.official-title: To amend title XXVII of the Public Health Service Act to establish civil liability for health insurance issuers with high levels of claims denials.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Patient Refunds for Bad Denials Act of 2026.

section.header: Establishing civil liability for health insurance issuers with high levels of claims denials

subsection.text: Title XXVII of the Public Health Service Act (42 U.S.C. 300gg et seq.) is amended by adding at the end the following new part:

section.header: Civil liability for high rates of claims denials

subsection.text: The Secretary may impose on each health insurance issuer offering group or individual health insurance coverage for a plan year beginning on or after January 1, 2027, a civil monetary penalty not to exceed the amount specified in subsection (e) if the Secretary finds that any such coverage offered by such issuer during such plan year had an claims denial percentage of 25 percent (or such lower percent as the Secretary may specify) or greater.

paragraph.text: For purposes of this section, the term claims denial percentage means, with respect to group or individual health insurance coverage and a plan year, the percentage of claims for items and services furnished during such plan year that the Secretary determines, pursuant to audits conducted under subsection (c), were denied.

A claim denied on the basis of fraud or lack of medical necessity that the Secretary determines, pursuant to audits conducted under subsection (c), were correctly denied shall not be treated as a denied claim for purposes of clause (i).

In assessing whether a claim that was denied by a health insurance issuer offering group or individual health insurance coverage on the basis of fraud was correctly denied for purposes of determining the claims denial percentage of such coverage, the Secretary shall find such claim to have been correctly denied only if such issuer provides sufficient information to the Secretary to demonstrate that such claim was fraudulent.

subsection.text: The Secretary may conduct such audits of group and individual health insurance coverage as the Secretary determines appropriate for purposes of ascertaining the claims denial percentage of such coverage.

The Secretary shall distribute on a pro rata basis to individuals enrolled during a plan year in group or individual health insurance coverage offered by a health insurance issuer which is subject to a civil monetary penalty imposed under this section with respect to such plan year an amount equal to amounts collected under this section for such penalties so imposed.

paragraph.text: For purposes of subsection (a), the amount specified in this subsection is, with respect to a health insurance issuer—

subparagraph.text: an additional $2,000,000 for every percentage point by which the claims denial percentage of such issuer exceeds 25 percent.

paragraph.text: The Secretary may adjust the amounts specified in this subsection for 2028 and each subsequent year to account for the change in the consumer price index for all urban consumers over the preceding year.

In determining the amount of a civil monetary penalty under this section, the Secretary may take into account any efforts made by the health insurance issuer to reduce the claims denial percentage of health insurance coverage offered by such issuer.
Subpart II of part A of title XXVII of the Public Health Service Act (42 U.S.C. 300gg–11 et seq.) is amended by adding at the end the following new section:

subsection.text: A health insurance issuer offering group or individual health insurance coverage shall, in the case such issuer denies a claim for an item or service furnished to an individual on the basis that such item or service was not medically necessary, provide to such individual a notice containing—

paragraph.text: the issuer’s medical necessity standards for such item or service; and
an explanation of why such item or service so furnished failed to meet such standards.

subsection.text: A health insurance issuer offering group or individual health insurance coverage shall for each plan year submit to the Secretary the percentage of claims that were denied under such coverage for any reason.

paragraph.text: The amendment made by this subsection shall apply with respect to plan years beginning on or after January 1, 2027.
